questions.assertion: The Board of Directors is the supreme authority in a company.
questions.reason: As per Section 166 of the Companies Act, 2013, the Board of Directors is responsible for managing the affairs of the company.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Board of Directors is indeed the supreme authority in a company and Section 166 of the Companies Act, 2013, emphasizes their responsibility in managing the company's affairs.

questions.assertion: The Chairman of the Board of Directors is a non-executive director.
questions.reason: In many companies, the Chairman of the Board of Directors is an independent director, who is not a part of the executive team.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Chairman of the Board of Directors is often a non-executive director and an independent director, who oversees the executive team.

questions.assertion: The Board of Directors has the power to appoint and remove the Managing Director.
questions.reason: As per Section 245 of the Companies Act, 2013, the Board of Directors has the authority to appoint and remove the Managing Director.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Board of Directors indeed has the power to appoint and remove the Managing Director, as per Section 245 of the Companies Act, 2013.

questions.assertion: The Managing Director is responsible for the day-to-day operations of the company.
questions.reason: As per Section 2(52) of the Companies Act, 2013, the Managing Director is responsible for the overall management of the company.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Managing Director is indeed responsible for the day-to-day operations of the company, as per Section 2(52) of the Companies Act, 2013.

questions.assertion: The Board of Directors meets at least once in a year.
questions.reason: As per Section 173 of the Companies Act, 2013, the Board of Directors is required to meet at least four times a year.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Board of Directors is indeed required to meet at least four times a year, as per Section 173 of the Companies Act, 2013, but it is not limited to just one annual meeting.

questions.assertion: The Board of Directors has the power to appoint an Executive Director as the Managing Director.
questions.reason: As per Section 2(51) of the Companies Act, 2013, the Board of Directors has the authority to appoint an Executive Director as the Managing Director.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Board of Directors indeed has the power to appoint an Executive Director as the Managing Director, as per Section 2(51) of the Companies Act, 2013.

questions.assertion: The Board of Directors is responsible for making strategic decisions for the company.
questions.reason: As per the principles of corporate governance, the Board of Directors is responsible for overseeing the overall strategy of the company.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Board of Directors indeed has a crucial role in making strategic decisions for the company, as per the principles of corporate governance.

questions.assertion: The Board of Directors has the power to raise funds for the company through public issue of shares.
questions.reason: As per Section 62 of the Companies Act, 2013, the Board of Directors has the authority to raise funds for the company through various means, including public issue of shares.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Board of Directors indeed has the power to raise funds for the company through public issue of shares, as per Section 62 of the Companies Act, 2013.

questions.assertion: The Board of Directors is responsible for overseeing the internal control systems of the company.
questions.reason: As per the principles of corporate governance, the Board of Directors is responsible for ensuring that the company has adequate internal control systems in place.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Board of Directors indeed has a crucial role in overseeing the internal control systems of the company, as per the principles of corporate governance.
